heading: Lighthouse and other sites; necessity and sufficiency of cession by State of jurisdiction
label: 549 Lighthouse and other sites; necessity and sufficiency of cession by State of jurisdiction

children.label: a
children.text: No lighthouse, beacon, public pier, or landmark, shall be built or erected on any site until cession of jurisdiction over the same has been made to the United States.

children.label: b
children.text: For the purposes of subsection (a), a cession by a State of jurisdiction over a place selected as the site of a lighthouse, or other structure or work referred to in subsection (a), shall be deemed sufficient if the cession contains a reservation that process issued under authority of such State may continue to be served within such place.

children.label: c
children.text: If no reservation of service described in subsection (b) is contained in a cession, all process may be served and executed within the place ceded, in the same manner as if no cession had been made.
